_comment: Data-driven style rules. Edit this file to ban/allow phrases without touching code. Patterns are JS regex source strings; flags are applied per pattern. Scope is the section the check runs against.

popularCultureScorecard._comment: The 'grading-rubric pretending to be prose' disease. Hard target. Scoped to popularCulture descriptions only — these MUST be lively interpretive prose, not a right/wrong checklist.

popularCultureWatch._comment: Softer scorecard tics that mutate from the banned set. Warn-level — flags the template before it hardens. 'captures'/'conveys'/'shows' only flagged as an evaluative hinge near a virtue noun.

allowlistStems._comment: SANCTIONED recurring framework phrases. The corpus repetition detector and template checks EXEMPT these — they are deliberate section architecture (Research/Educational scaffolding), not model tics. Per the user's correction: do NOT police these.

repetitionStems._comment: Corpus repetition detector config. Scans freeform interpretive sections for 4-5 word evaluative stems that recur across many entries and are NOT on the allowlist. This is the permanent anti-mole: it surfaces the NEXT tic by frequency, so you ban or allowlist it without knowing it in advance.

plainEnglish._comment: CEFR B1 'Plain English' layer gates (validators/plainEnglish.cjs). The layer is generated FROM content.beginner by transformation. Numeric thresholds are the drift fence; tune them here, not in code. Locked after the pilot — see PLAIN_ENGLISH_SPEC.md. Readability is two-tier: fkWarn is the B1 target ceiling (warn), fkMax is the hard fail (clearly above B1). Entries with unavoidable polysyllabic domain terms (electricity, photosynthesis) ride higher on FK, so the hard fail sits at 8.0. Lengths are word counts. lengthBand = total B1 words allowed as a fraction of Beginner words.
plainEnglish._matterCapComment: Max evaluative 'matters/mattered' uses allowed in one B1 block (the register crutch). Counts the verb/eval frames only, NOT the physical-substance noun ('states of matter', 'dark matter'). Plain-English-ONLY for now; the published-canon overuse (avg ~2.9/entry) is a parked future fix. Tighten to 1 to be stricter.
plainEnglish._bannedComment: FAIL-level non-B1 phrasings. High-precision only — keep false positives near zero; the idiom long-tail is caught by the review agent, not regex.
plainEnglish._watchComment: WARN-level idiom/phrasal-verb watchlist. Flags a likely-non-B1 construction for review; promote a recurring one to bannedPatterns.
